name: Wayne County CARES Emergency Shelter
eligibility: Wayne County residents 18+; youth 14+ with guardian consent or emancipated
intake_process: Walk in or call for intake screening; bring ID if available

name: Covenant House Michigan
address: 2959 Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Detroit, MI 48208
intake_process: Walk in or call crisis line; no appointment needed for emergency intake

name: Gleaners Community Food Bank
hours: Mon–Fri 8am–4pm; Food pantry hours vary by location
eligibility: Any Wayne County resident in need; no income verification required at pantry
intake_process: Visit partner pantry, show proof of address; find locations at gcfb.org/find-food

name: Monroe County Community Action Agency – Food Pantry
eligibility: Monroe County residents; income guidelines apply (up to 200% federal poverty level)
intake_process: Call ahead or walk in; bring proof of address and ID

name: Detroit Health Department – Youth Sexual Health Clinic
eligibility: Youth and adults; confidential services for those under 18 available
intake_process: Walk-in or call for appointment; insurance not required

name: Henry Ford Health – Community Health Navigator Program
eligibility: Open to all; sliding scale fees; Medicaid and Medicare accepted
intake_process: Call to connect with a health navigator who will schedule appropriate services

name: Michigan Legal Help – Wayne County Self-Help Center
eligibility: Any Michigan resident; free legal information and referrals
intake_process: Walk in or call; online resources available 24/7 at michiganlegalhelp.org

name: National Runaway Safeline
hours: 24/7 crisis line, chat, and text support
eligibility: Runaway and at-risk youth up to age 21; families of runaway youth
intake_process: Call, text, or chat 24/7; no information required to access immediate help

name: Monroe County Community Mental Health – Crisis Services
hours: 24/7 crisis line; walk-in crisis center Mon–Fri 8am–5pm
eligibility: Monroe County residents; crisis services open to all regardless of ability to pay
intake_process: Call crisis line anytime; walk in during business hours; emergency services available after hours

name: United Way 211 – Michigan
eligibility: Any Michigan resident seeking help; specialists available in multiple languages
intake_process: Dial or text 211; describe your need and a specialist will connect you to local resources
